response: The global food system faces major strain as the population is projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, requiring a 60% increase in food production while climate change reduces yields through droughts, floods, and extreme heat. Agriculture also contributes about 25% of greenhouse gas emissions, while water scarcity, aquifer depletion, and soil degradation—affecting 33% of arable land—threaten productivity. Roughly one-third of all food produced is lost or wasted, worsening the challenge. Potential solutions include AI-driven precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant crops, regenerative farming, and policy reforms, but they must be scaled while keeping food affordable and accessible.
